extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož attacked on the final steep climb in Trevi, held off YATES Adam's late counter by 3 seconds to win stage 3.
tactical_patterns: Roglič attacked solo early on final steep climb rather than waiting for closer summit, gambling on power to hold late chasers.
Moscon attempted bridge to Roglič but ended up dragging Yates/Thomas to close gap, neutralizing his own Sky team's advantage.
Van Avermaert initiated GC group move but faded, suggesting classics specialists vulnerable on sustained steep gradients even when fresh.
Majka lost significant time in mid-race crash with Bora teammates Buchmann/Geschke, exposing vulnerability of isolated team leaders on rolling stages.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: held solo attack on sustained steep finish against superior climbers, revealing diesel power advantage over punchier GC rivals
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: closed massive gap to Roglič in final kilometer despite late start, showing explosive acceleration but poor race positioning
rider_signals.MOSCON Gianni: negative: countered Van Avermaert but served as pace bridge for rivals Thomas/Yates rather than isolating them for teammate
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: took race lead on countback but passive tactics sitting on wheels suggests conserving rather than dominating
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: negative: lost GC contention in mid-race crash clustered with own Bora teammates, indicating poor team positioning protocol
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: positive: finished third just days after Strade Bianche win, suggesting exceptional form resilience across varied terrain
